prevalence.source: IHME GBD (bipolar disorder) + subtype distribution syntheses
prevalence.trend: stable; often underdiagnosed as recurrent depression

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: DSM-5/ICD-11: hypomanic episodes + major depressive episodes; no history of full manic episode

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: low; frequently misclassified as unipolar depression
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Variable; relapse common without maintenance therapy

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Requires careful history for hypomania; often identified in specialist settings
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Mood stabilizers (e.g., lamotrigine/lithium) and atypical antipsychotics; avoid antidepressant monotherapy when possible
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Underdiagnosis and inappropriate antidepressant monotherapy in some settings
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Access to core mood stabilizers and specialist care in major centers
